transcript.speaker: Priya
transcript.text: Morning everyone. Okay let me start by closing yesterday's action items before we go into new stuff. Vikram — env got fixed at what time yesterday?

transcript.speaker: Vikram
transcript.text: Infra applied the config at 1:20pm. I confirmed it at 1:30 and pinged Neha immediately.

transcript.speaker: Priya
transcript.text: Good. Suresh — gateway endpoint and schema, did that go to Ananya?

transcript.speaker: Suresh
transcript.text: Yes. Endpoint verified — we were using the wrong URL actually. I fixed it and shared the corrected schema with Ananya around 2pm.

transcript.speaker: Ananya
transcript.text: Got it and updated the field mapping. Mobile alignment is also fixed. That's done.

transcript.speaker: Priya
transcript.text: Okay good. Now Neha — you messaged at midnight. What's happening?

transcript.speaker: Neha
transcript.text: So I started running the test suite from 2pm yesterday. The environment is up but we're getting random timeouts on the payment initiation call. Not every time — roughly 1 in 5 requests is timing out. It's very inconsistent, which is why I kept running till late to see if it was a pattern.

transcript.speaker: Rahul
transcript.text: 1 in 5 is basically 20% failure rate. That's not acceptable for go-live. Suresh, any idea what's causing it?

transcript.speaker: Suresh
transcript.text: I think I know. Our timeout config is set to 3 seconds. But yesterday when I was reading the gateway integration doc properly — their SLA says the first authentication call can take up to 8 seconds sometimes. We've been setting an unrealistically short timeout.

transcript.speaker: Neha
transcript.text: See! I said yesterday also it didn't feel like an infra issue. The pattern was not random — it was happening more on the first call in a session.

transcript.speaker: Vikram
transcript.text: Yes I also didn't think it was infra side. The connection is stable. 3 second timeout is application level config, that's Suresh's side.

transcript.speaker: Suresh
transcript.text: I'm changing it to 10 seconds right now. But there's also another issue — we have no retry logic. If the call times out, the error just gets thrown directly to frontend. User sees nothing useful.

transcript.speaker: Ananya
transcript.text: Yes, right now it's a blank red screen. I added a generic error message last night but it's not good enough. We need proper retry with user feedback — like 'processing, please wait'.

transcript.speaker: Rajan
transcript.text: How long to fix both of these properly?

transcript.speaker: Rahul
transcript.text: Timeout change is 30 minutes. Basic retry — Suresh maybe 2-3 hours? Proper graceful handling with UI feedback — end of day.

transcript.speaker: Suresh
transcript.text: Yes that's about right.

transcript.speaker: Rajan
transcript.text: Client wants a demo today at 3pm. They called this morning and asked. Can we show them something?

transcript.speaker: Priya
transcript.text: Sir we can do a controlled demo. We run only the happy path — the scenarios that are passing. We don't show the edge cases. But I want to be honest with you — it is not fully production ready yet.

transcript.speaker: Rajan
transcript.text: Okay. Do the demo carefully. Rahul — this retry issue needs to be done today. Not tomorrow. Wednesday go-live is still the target.

transcript.speaker: Rahul
transcript.text: Understood. Suresh, Ananya — can we do a war room after this call? The three of us, just finish it together.

transcript.speaker: Suresh
transcript.text: Let me check calendar... yes I'm free.

transcript.speaker: Ananya
transcript.text: Same. I'll keep the error handling code open.

transcript.speaker: Priya
transcript.text: Neha — can you rerun the happy path suite before 2:30pm so we have fresh results for the demo? Just the passing scenarios.

transcript.speaker: Neha
transcript.text: Yes. Once Suresh deploys the timeout fix I'll rerun. Will share results by 2pm.
